text.title: Daily Ads Anomaly Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules anchoring the daily anomaly triage. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every anomaly check MUST declare a numeric threshold (e.g. spend > 1.5× 7d avg) before the run. Thresholds with only qualitative "high" / "abnormal" labels are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Qualitative thresholds drift between days; numeric thresholds make the log comparable across runs and owners.

rule.statement: Every triggered anomaly MUST produce one of {pause, reduce-budget, raise-budget, escalate, no-op} and a named owner handle. "Investigate later" is not an action.
rule.rationale: Without a named action + owner, anomalies persist for days.

rule.statement: Pauses initiated by the daily run MUST be reversible by default (auto-resume in 24h) unless the same anomaly tripped 2 days in a row or daily cost exceeds a hard cap.
rule.rationale: Single-day spikes are often noise; aggressive pauses kill compounding campaigns. Hard caps prevent runaway loss.

rule.statement: The daily-run log MUST record account-id + ad-set-id + time-window + threshold values + actual values + action + owner. Logs missing any field are rejected by the validator.
rule.rationale: Without context, today's "pause" is tomorrow's "why is this off?". Full context = silent re-classification impossible.

rule.statement: The same anomaly on the same ad-set 2 consecutive days MUST escalate to a named human (handle + Slack thread). Auto-pause is allowed; auto-disable of a campaign is forbidden without escalation.
rule.rationale: Persistent anomalies often have structural causes (creative fatigue, targeting drift) needing human judgment.
